commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  redirects outside /root/hermes, package removal, disk writes, fork bombs

* docs: add phase 15 task YAML — scheduling engine constraints + SF + conflict detection

4 stages, 5 groups (A-E), 37 tasks with exit criteria.
Stage 1: Constraint types + SF in Rust engine (single agent)
Stage 2: Cascade/graph/conflict + TS types (2 parallel agents)
Stage 3: Constraint UI + conflict indicators (single agent)
Stage 4: Integration tests + validation (single agent)

commit.message:
Replace Babel with Sucrase, add dev watcher, component library

Tier 1 changes:

1. Replace @babel/standalone (2.5 MB) with Sucrase (215 KB)
   - Bundle: 3.1 MB → 444 KB (85% reduction)
   - API: async → sync (no lazy loading needed)
   - Speed: ~16x faster transpilation
   - Removed BabelManager.ts, lazyLoadBabel setting
   - All 46 tests pass with Sucrase

2. Dev watcher with auto-deploy
   - `npm run dev` now watches src/ and auto-rebuilds
   - Auto-copies main.js, manifest.json, styles.css to vault
   - Reads deploy path from .vault_plugin_dir file
   - Production build (`npm run build`) still one-shot

3. Component library (vault-plant/components/)
   - 9 file-based components: StatusBadge, Card, DataRow, Metric,
     ProgressBar, Overlay, Modal, Toast, AlarmRow
   - Set "components" in plugin settings to enable
   - Available in any jsx block without re-defining

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: serialize Discord IDs as strings to prevent JS precision loss

Discord snowflake IDs exceed Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (2^53). When the API
returned them as JSON numbers, JS JSON.parse rounded them, causing batch
member lookup to fail with wrong IDs (showing Unknown instead of names).

- schemas.py: DiscordId Annotated type — accepts int|str, serializes as str
- Apply DiscordId to all discord_id/user_id/channel_id fields in schemas
- KickTargets/StackingPairs/Users: remove Number() conversion in payloads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(video): api_client, generator, dawn_mood_call config, scripts & tests (#34)

* spec(014): multi-agent video production architecture

Define 6-agent video planning system with:
- Story Writer: narrative arc, visual anchor
- Copywriter: hooks, captions, CTA
- Stitch Planner: production method selection (NEW)
- Prompt Engineer: service-specific prompts
- Reviewer: quality gate
- Orchestrator: flow control

Key design decisions:
- Agent order: stitch_planner before prompt_engineer (generation method determines prompt type)
- Marketing layer extension points: campaign_context, performance_hints, experiment_vars
- StitchPlan/AssetManifest contracts defined
- VideoPlan extension strategy: Option A (extend model)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec(014): add implementation details for P0-P3

- P0-1: Stitch Planner references SERVICE_CONSTRAINTS
- P0-2: Agent order with fan-out (copywriter || prompt_engineer)
- P0-3: Cascade invalidation logic
- P1-1: Reviewer integrates with VideoPlanScorer
- P1-2: ShotDraft → VideoShot mapping with notes preservation
- P1-3: Error handling with safe_node wrapper
- P2-1: Cost/token tracking (llm_calls, token_budget)
- P2-2: Prompt file loading via extended prompt_loader
- P2-3: Account config with visual_settings and channels
- P3: LangGraph dependency (uv add langgraph)

Updated VideoAgentState with new fields and added v3 change history.

* plan(014): multi-agent video implementation plan

5-Phase implementation plan for multi-agent video production system:
- Phase 1: Foundation (state, schemas, graph structure)
- Phase 2: Core Production Flow (story_writer, stitch_planner, prompt_engineer)
- Phase 3: Copy & Review Loop (copywriter, reviewer, orchestrator)
- Phase 4: Integration (VideoGenerator, VideoPlan extension)
- Phase 5: Testing & Polish

Key components:
- VideoAgentState TypedDict with marketing extension points
- ShotDraft, StitchPlan, AssetItem schemas
- Cascade invalidation in orchestrator
- Fan-out parallel execution (copywriter || prompt_engineer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(014): address plan review issues

Critical fixes:
- Add narrative_transition to ShotDraft (story-to-stitch connection)
- Add reviewer → orchestrator edge (graph was broken)
- Fix route_by_revision for budget_exceeded and max_iterations
- Add prompt loader (prompts/loader.py)
- Add account_config initialization in VideoAgentGraph.generate()
- Document cascade re-execution tradeoff

Spec updates:
- narrative_transition field added to ShotDraft schema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix MCP protocol: wrap responses in JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope for Claude Code compatibility

The /mcp endpoint was returning raw results, but Claude Code's HTTP MCP transport
expects JSON-RPC 2.0 format (jsonrpc, id, result fields). Added backward-compatible
rpcWrap helper that only adds the envelope when jsonrpc:"2.0" is present in the request.
Dashboard REST endpoints (GET /tools, POST /tools/:name) are unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Android Canvas rendering with Perry StringHeader FFI

Implement full editor rendering on Android via HoneEditorView JNI callbacks.
Replace CStr::from_ptr with str_from_header for Perry's NaN-boxed string
pointers. Implement cache_line/set_viewport protocol with packed token
parsing, line cache, and per-token colored text via Canvas.drawText.
Add .cargo/config.toml for Android NDK cross-compilation. Include iOS
editor view and text renderer improvements.

commit.message:
fix: use fixed-position dropdowns in FacetSidebar and guard wildcard facet labels

The sidebar's overflow-y:auto clipped pointer events on the absolutely-
positioned dropdown menu, making Expand All, Collapse All, and Manage
Filters unclickable. Switch both sidebar dropdowns to position:fixed
with getBoundingClientRect positioning and native mousedown
stopPropagation — matching the pattern already used by FilterPillBar
and SubscriptionFilter.

Also guard against wildcard factSheetType ("*") in facetKeyToLabel so
relation facets with a wildcard target fall through to the camelCase
fallback instead of producing "*s".

commit.message:
security: full hardening pass — path sandbox, shell injection, rate limiting, headers

bridge.py:
- Path sandboxing: _safe_path() restricts all file tool access to /root/hermes
- shell=False + shlex.split() on run_command (shell injection fix)
- BUILD_TOKEN now required (returns 401 if unset, not open-by-default)
- Prompt injection delimiters: visitor input wrapped in <visitor_message> tags
- LLM output XSS: <script> tags stripped from render() before serving
- Visitor session full UUID (was 8-char truncated)
- Session TTL: 1hr expiry with 5min cleanup background task
- chmod 0o600 on chat_history.json after every write
- Startup warnings if BUILD_TOKEN or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset
- import time added

static/landing.html:
- iframe sandbox attribute added

run + install.ps1:
- Hardened nginx config: server_tokens off, all 5 security headers,
  rate limiting (10r/m ask, 1r/m build), 404 for sensitive files,
  index landing.html, localhost-only operator endpoints
- Rate limit zones injected into nginx.conf http block
- chmod 600 on .env in all code paths
- Auto-generate BUILD_TOKEN if not present in .env

.env.example:
- BUILD_TOKEN documented as required (was commented out)

commit.message:
Serve skills.md with server-side URL substitution

Add a Next.js route handler at /agents/skills.md that reads the template
from public/agents/skills.md, replaces __API_URL__ and __FRONTEND_URL__
placeholders with values derived from NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and the request
host, then serves as text/plain. This way a self-hosted deployment
automatically serves the correct API and frontend URLs without hardcoding.

analytics.py, and logs GSC callback failures to system error log.
